records.ety: [L. custodia, fr. custos guard; prob. akin to Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ to hide, and E. hide. Seee Hide to cover.]

defs: A keeping or guarding; care, watch, inspection, for keeping, preservation, or security.
quotes: A fleet of thirty ships for the custody of the narrow seas.
Jailer, take him to thy custody.

defs: State of being guarded and watched to prevent escape; restraint of liberty; confinement; imprisonment.
quotes: What pease will be given To us enslaved, but custody severe, And stripes and arbitrary punishment?
